name: Four Seasons
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts — the Canadian-founded ultra-luxury hospitality brand that built its empire on service consistency across 100+ properties worldwide. Canvas is warm parchment-ivory (#f7f3eb) or velvet-black for evening-mode landing pages. Type runs a refined serif (Trajan / Cormorant / a custom display serif) at editorial scale — never sans-serif at hero. Photography is travel-magazine grade: cinematic full-bleed property shots with golden-hour light, infinity pools photographed from drone height, lobby chandeliers in candlelit warmth. The voice is whispered luxury — never loud, never claiming, always implying. The wordmark sits at the top in a serif lockup that resembles a luxury watch dial more than a hotel sign.

design_language.motion_signature: Cinematic full-bleed. Hero videos auto-play silent: drone shots of resort properties, gentle pans across infinity pools, candlelit lobby reveals. Cross-fades are slow (~2s) — luxury is measured by how long the brand can hold a single image. Hover states change opacity faintly, no scale, no shadow.

trademark_signals: Parchment-ivory canvas (#f7f3eb) with serif typography at every tier — the brand voice is 'old-world luxury' rendered in modern web layout
Trajan / custom-serif wordmark — refusing the modern grotesk convention every other hotel chain has adopted
Full-bleed cinematic property photography — golden-hour drone shots, infinity pools, candlelit interiors — the photography IS the brand voltage
Whispered luxury voice — copy reads 'A sanctuary above the city' rather than 'Book now and save 20%'
Property-page hero with property-name in serif at 80–120px, followed by a single landscape photo — refusing the multi-photo grid every other booking site uses
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute Trajan with a modern grotesk. The Roman-inscriptional serif IS the brand voice — sans-serif reads as 'modern boutique hotel', not 'global ultra-luxury'
Don't add bright accent colors. The brand is parchment + ink + restrained gold; saturated CTAs read as deals-site, not luxury hospitality
Don't use multi-photo property grids. Each property gets a cinematic single-photo hero; collages read as off-brand
Don't write marketing-bullet copy. Property descriptions are atmospheric long paragraphs about light, ocean, dining, terrace — never feature lists
Don't show booking-discount stickers. Promotional pricing is off-brand; the brand never competes on price
